title: Nudity in the manga
wikitext:
[]: Nudity in the manga.             Quote Post by Caleb89sw » Fri Apr 26, 2013 8:14 pm Hey. Sorry if this is inappropriate for this thread. I'm new to Evangelion and I was just wondering, how much and how explicit is the nudity in Sakamoto's manga? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 26, 2013 8:53 pm It's nothing to freak out about. All it is is that they can draw nipples because it's not on TV. At times I think it's actually less sexually explicit than the show. I'd give you an example, but I wouldn't want to ruin any of the differences for you.

[]:             Quote Post by Caleb89sw » Fri Apr 26, 2013 9:15 pm Thanks for your response. Oh, that's ok. I don't have many resources to see the series, so I'm not too concerned about spoilers or anything. I saw Evangelion 1.11 and have read a couple volumes from my library, as well as looked up bits and pieces of the story on sites like Wikipedia and this site, so I already know a little about the series. If you or anyone else wants to give an example to get your point across, go ahead. I was just wondering if I made a blind buy what kind of content there would be.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 26, 2013 9:26 pm Caleb89sw wrote: If you or anyone else wants to give an example to get your point across, go ahead. I was just wondering if I made a blind buy what kind of content there would be.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Apr 27, 2013 7:45 pm He's not scared of finding nudity; he's asking where to find it. (Sorry, someone had to say it)

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Sat Apr 27, 2013 7:57 pm I haven't read the manga in a couple years. So take what I say with a grain of salt. In the way of boobage, there's a fair bit. There's nothing truly explicit, though. user-02 wrote: He's not scared of finding nudity; he's asking where to find it. (Sorry, someone had to say it)

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Wed May 01, 2013 2:50 pm You get to see Shinji's wiener pretty clearly.
